skill.name: deploy-model
skill.description: Routes Azure OpenAI model deployment requests to preset, customized, or capacity-discovery workflows for Azure AI Foundry projects.
skill.summary: Deploy Azure OpenAI models through guided Azure AI Foundry workflows.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found no evidence of malicious intent, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, weak cryptography, or filesystem traversal. Most flagged items are Markdown examples, Azure CLI guidance, a relative documentation link, or helper scripts that generate an Azure AI Foundry portal URL. The remaining risk is medium because the skill guides authenticated Azure deployment actions that can create billable cloud resources.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Azure Deployment Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill routes users through Azure OpenAI deployment workflows and requires confirmation before deployment. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can create billable Azure resources when used with authenticated credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly covers deployment mode selection, project resolution, and pre-deployment validation. The confirmation requirement reduces risk, but authenticated cloud changes remain operationally sensitive.

medium_findings.title: Helper Script Processes User-Supplied Identifiers
medium_findings.description: The Bash helper accepts subscription, resource group, project, and deployment names, then uses shell command substitution to encode the subscription ID and print a portal URL. The arguments are quoted and no command injection was confirmed, but identifier validation would reduce malformed URL risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static command-substitution finding is real, but the semantic use is limited to local string transformation and URL output. No eval, remote request, or unquoted command construction was found.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Azure Portal URL Is Not Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to the Azure AI Foundry portal and is only concatenated into a link that is printed or returned. No evidence found of data being sent to that URL by the scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both scripts build a portal URL as output rather than performing a network request. The destination is an expected Microsoft Azure domain for this deployment skill.

low_findings.title: Markdown Relative Link Is Not Filesystem Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts correspond to a Markdown link that points readers to a quota skill document. No evidence found of runtime file access using that relative path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged sequence appears inside documentation text as a relative link. It is not used by code that reads, writes, or executes files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts occur in descriptive deployment text, headings, and prerequisite sections. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithms being used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found Azure OpenAI deployment instructions, not cryptographic API use. The scanner likely matched substrings in ordinary documentation.

low_findings.title: Test Prompt Reconnaissance Alert Is Benign
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert points to a negative test case for an invalid Azure region. No evidence found of host, network, or environment reconnaissance instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is part of a table of expected failure cases. It tests graceful handling of an invalid region rather than collecting system information.

dangerous_patterns.title: Azure CLI Commands in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run Azure CLI commands for model catalog and quota validation. This is expected behavior, but it should run only in the user-approved Azure context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and relevant to deployment validation. The risk depends on the user environment and permissions rather than malicious code in the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in URL Helper
dangerous_patterns.description: The Bash helper uses command substitution with standard utilities to transform a subscription GUID. Review found no command injection vector in the quoted pipeline, but this pattern deserves review in community scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern exists exactly where static analysis flagged it, but the command pipeline is fixed and user data is quoted. The finding is a moderate review concern, not confirmed exploitation.

content.value_statement: Azure OpenAI deployment requires choosing the right project, region, SKU, capacity, and safety policy. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through preset, customized, and capacity-aware Azure AI Foundry workflows.
actual_capabilities: Routes simple deployment requests to a preset Azure OpenAI deployment workflow.
Routes custom requests to guided version, SKU, capacity, and RAI policy selection.
Identifies capacity discovery requests before deployment decisions are made.
Requires target project confirmation before any deployment action.
Directs validation of model SKU support and available subscription quota.
Includes helper scripts that generate Azure AI Foundry deployment portal URLs.
limitations: Requires Azure CLI authentication and permissions in the target subscription.
Does not list or delete existing model deployments.
Depends on separate preset, customize, and capacity sub-skills for full workflows.
Helper URL scripts do not deploy resources or verify portal link availability.

use_cases.title: Quick Model Deployment
use_cases.description: Deploy a common Azure OpenAI model with defaults after confirming the target project and region.

use_cases.title: Controlled Production Setup
use_cases.description: Choose model version, SKU, capacity, and content filtering before creating a deployment.

use_cases.title: Capacity-Aware Planning
use_cases.description: Find regions and projects with enough quota before selecting a deployment path.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy With Defaults
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy gpt-4o to my Azure AI Foundry project using recommended defaults. Confirm the project before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Deployment Settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me deploy o3-mini with custom version, SKU, capacity, and content filter settings.

prompt_templates.title: Find Available Capacity
prompt_templates.prompt: Check which regions can support a gpt-4o deployment with 100K TPM, then recommend the best deployment path.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Production Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate model support, quota, region, project, SKU, and RAI policy before deploying gpt-4o for production use.

output_examples.output: The assistant identifies a preset deployment request, confirms the Azure AI Foundry project, validates model and quota, then proceeds only after user approval.

output_examples.input: Deploy gpt-4o with 50K capacity and custom settings.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks capacity first, presents eligible regions or projects, then routes the user into a customized deployment flow.
The assistant treats the request as capacity discovery and returns deployment options instead of creating a model immediately.

best_practices: Confirm the Azure project, region, and resource group before starting deployment.
Validate model SKU support and available quota before presenting selectable options.
Use capacity discovery first when the prompt includes a capacity target or best-region requirement.
anti_patterns: Do not deploy to a default project without showing the target to the user.
Do not present hardcoded SKU lists when live Azure catalog data is required.
Do not use this skill to list, delete, or manage existing deployments.

faq.question: Does this skill create Azure resources?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide deployment creation when the user has authenticated Azure CLI access and confirms the target project.

faq.question: Can it choose the best deployment region?
faq.answer: Yes. It routes best-region and capacity questions to discovery before deployment when capacity requirements are present.

faq.question: Can it customize model version and SKU?
faq.answer: Yes. Prompts with customization keywords are routed to a guided configuration flow.

faq.question: Does it delete deployments?
faq.answer: No. The skill description explicitly excludes deletion and listing of existing deployments.

faq.question: What tools does it require?
faq.answer: It requires Azure CLI authentication and Azure permissions for the target subscription and Foundry project.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The reviewed files do not show malicious behavior, but authenticated cloud deployment can affect cost and production resources.
